scenario_name: Scenario 15 — Concurrency Bug in Async JavaScript
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 500 simultaneous DB calls / connection pool exhaustion clearly identified as the primary critical finding, (2) concurrency limiting recommended with p-limit example and tunable cap, (3) explicitly calls out that the JS increment is NOT a race condition ('Race condition is NOT an issue here'), (4) TypeScript err.message type safety noted as a critical finding with instanceof guard fix, (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Correctly identifies unbounded concurrency / connection pool exhaustion as the real risk, not a false JS race condition
Explicitly debunks the race condition misconception in important_issues, explaining JS single-threaded event loop semantics
Provides concrete p-limit fix with code example and reasonable default concurrency cap of 20
TypeScript unknown-typed catch variable identified with proper instanceof Error guard
safe_to_deploy is false with clear rationale about cascading failures
Bonus: identifies per-delivery timeout risk (hung promise blocks entire batch) which is a legitimate additional concern
Bonus: suggests Promise.allSettled as defense-in-depth alternative
